title: She knit an MBTA-themed sweater inspired by her commutes. The internet is loving it.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston designer Megan Lam created an MBTA-themed sweater featuring trains from the commuter rail, Orange, Green, and Red lines.
Lam's sweater, inspired by her MBTA commutes, received positive attention after being shared on Instagram last week.
The sweater design reflects Lam's personal experiences and her unique approach, foregoing patterns for a one-of-a-kind art piece.

title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Emmie Laudano, 18, from Wellesley, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Tierney Learning Center.
Laudano has been training with the Dreamfar program and preparing on Heartbreak Hill each Saturday.
Funds raised through Laudano's marathon run will support educational resources for kids at the Tierney Learning Center.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com features a quiz to test knowledge on recent Greater Boston news stories.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019, curated the Greater Boston news quiz.
The quiz covers trending local stories to help readers stay informed about Greater Boston events.

title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins lost 2-1 to the shorthanded Florida Panthers, with coach Marco Sturm citing a lack of respect for the opponent.
Despite dominating strong teams, the Bruins have struggled with lower-ranked opponents, like Florida, near the season's end.
Fraser Minten scored Boston's only goal, while Sergei Bobrovsky blocked 14 shots in the third period to secure Florida's victory.

title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied, 40, from Reading, sentenced for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts, violating the Lacey Act.
Bied ordered parts from Cameroon and Indonesia, including skulls of orangutans and leopards, smuggling them into the U.S.
Bied to serve eight months in prison, pay $75,000 fine, and two years supervised release for smuggling wildlife parts.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black of Taunton was convicted of first-degree murder for stabbing 19-year-old Milteer Hendricks in Falmouth.
The attack occurred on June 10, 2023, outside a Falmouth apartment where Black and four others confronted Hendricks.
Black will be sentenced on Friday, facing mandatory life imprisonment without parole for the murder conviction.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia was suspended with pay following an investigative report by the Middlesex District Attorney's Office.
The investigation focused on Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged drunk driving crash on Dec. 12, 2023, which led to a fatality.
Prosecutors alleged DeLucia instructed an official to keep Quigley's suspected intoxication level confidential.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker sentenced to 3-5 years for manslaughter of foster child in Adams, MA.
The infant died in 2020 from untreated medical conditions while in the couple's care, according to prosecutors.
The couple's sentences are staggered due to having four other children, with each serving probation post-prison.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, sentenced to life without parole for fatally poisoning boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in 2022.
Fowler, 55, died from ethylene glycol poisoning; Church's jealousy over Fowler's affair was a motive, prosecutors stated.
Investigators found antifreeze in Fowler's Powerade bottle; Church's DNA was confirmed on the bottle, court documents revealed.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox start the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst since 2019.
Boston's pitching staff struggles, ranking last in MLB for home runs allowed per nine innings.
Key batters like Willson Contreras and Trevor Story deliver poor performances, exacerbating offensive woes.
